perturbation.original_text: LMG grants TAG a worldwide, nonexclusive, irrevocable, perpetual license to load, execute, access, employ, use, store, or display ("Use") the object code version of the LMG Tools and Documentation (the "License") for the period specified in Exhibit A in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: LMG grants TAG a worldwide, nonexclusive, revocable, temporary license to load, execute, access, employ, use, store, or display ("Use") the object code version of the LMG Tools and Documentation (the "License") for the period specified in Exhibit A in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. This license can be terminated by LMG at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the license is 'irrevocable, perpetual' creating a right that cannot be taken away once granted; however, the modified version introduces 'revocable, temporary' and 'can be terminated by LMG' creating a contradiction around the terms of usage. This will create uncertainty around the terms of usage as it would not be clear to TAG if the license can be canceled anytime.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE II LICENSE OF LMG TOOLS
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE II LICENSE OF LMG TOOLS
perturbation.contradicted_text: LMG grants TAG a worldwide, nonexclusive, revocable, temporary license to load, execute, access, employ, use, store, or display ("Use") the object code version of the LMG Tools and Documentation (the "License") for the period specified in Exhibit A in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. This license can be terminated by LMG at any time with 30 days written notice. TAG may Use the LMG Tools and Documentation solely to provide TPA Services to or for the New Customers and Terminated Customers, and perform its obligations under the Administrative Services and Teaming Agreement, New Customer Contracts and Subcontracts including performing disaster recovery, disaster testing, and backup as TAG deems necessary. The License grant includes a license under all current and future patents owned by or licensed to LMG that are applicable to the LMG Tools and Documentation or the provision or receipt of the LMG Services, to the extent necessary to exercise any of the foregoing rights. LMG acknowledges and agrees that the New Customers may have access to and Use of the LMG Tools and Documentation under the terms of the New Customer Contracts. The License also includes the right to Use the source code version of LMG Tools in accordance with the terms and conditions of Section 3.7. Except as specified in this Agreement, the License does not permit TAG to sublicense, rent or allow third parties to Use the LMG Tools or Documentation.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted an 'irrevocable, perpetual' license. The changed text modifies this to a 'revocable, temporary' license that 'can be terminated by LMG.' This directly contradicts the concept of an irrevocable and perpetual license, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text also states that the license can be terminated, but the changed text introduces new terms that affect the whole document. This contradiction creates uncertainty and potential legal disputes regarding the duration and termination rights associated with the license, impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Customer Data is and shall remain the property of TAG and/or the applicable New Customers.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer Data is and shall remain the property of LMG until the end of the term, after which it shall become the property of TAG and/or the applicable New Customers.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that 'Customer Data is and shall remain the property of TAG', clarifying the immediate ownership of customer data. The modification inserts 'Customer Data is and shall remain the property of LMG until the end of the term', directly contradicting the original statement and creates an ambiguity about who owns the data and when the data will pass over to TAG.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Customer Data is and shall remain the property of LMG until the end of the term, after which it shall become the property of TAG and/or the applicable New Customers. LMG shall promptly deliver Customer Data (or the portion of such Customer Data specified by TAG) to TAG in the format and on the media in which it exists as of the date of the request or in such other format or media as TAG reasonably requests. Upon TAG's request, LMG shall return, destroy or securely erase, as directed by TAG, all copies of the Customer Data in LMG's possession or under LMG's control within thirty (30) business days. LMG shall not withhold Customer Data under any circumstance including as a means of resolving a dispute. Customer Data shall not be utilized by LMG for any purpose other than the performance of the LMG Services and shall not be sold, assigned, leased, commercially exploited or otherwise provided to third parties by or on behalf of LMG or any LMG Personnel. LMG shall not possess or assert any lien or other right against or to Customer Data.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'Customer Data is and shall remain the property of TAG'. The changed text introduces a period where the data is the property of LMG, directly contradicting the original ownership statement, and creates an ambiguity about who owns the data and when. The 'Contradicted Text' is actually the same as the 'Changed Text' because the structural flaw of having two clauses in the contract on Customer Data, with conflicting implications, creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: LMG shall provide the hosting services through a dedicated telecommunications connection to TAG.
perturbation.changed_text: LMG shall provide the hosting services through a telecommunications connection to TAG, and the type of connection (dedicated or shared) will be determined at LMG's sole discretion based on bandwidth availability and cost considerations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a 'dedicated telecommunications connection to TAG,' which sets a clear standard for the type of connection. The change alters this by stating, 'the type of connection (dedicated or shared) will be determined at LMG's sole discretion,' contradicting the explicit dedicated connection provision. This shift introduces uncertainty about the reliability and quality of the connection as TAG is expecting a dedicated connection based on the original text.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE III SUPPORT AND HOSTING SERVICES
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III SUPPORT AND HOSTING SERVICES
perturbation.contradicted_text: Hosting Services. LMG shall host the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software at its existing data center facility in Petaluma, California for the period specified in Exhibit A. Except for downtime for scheduled maintenance, LMG shall make the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software available to users between 7:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. Eastern Time. Notice of scheduled maintenance shall be provided to TAG via email at least ten (10) days before the scheduled maintenance. LMG shall be financially and operationally responsible for the hosting environment including maintenance, repair, replacement and upgrade, and the performance, availability, reliability, compatibility and interoperability of the LMG Tools, CSC Software, Other Third Party Software and hosting environment. LMG shall provide the hosting services through a telecommunications connection to TAG, and the type of connection (dedicated or shared) will be determined at LMG's sole discretion based on bandwidth availability and cost considerations. The equipment, connectivity and other items located at LMG's existing data center facility in Petaluma, California that is described in Exhibit C to the Administrative Services and Teaming Agreement are included within the hosting environment and access to be provided by LMG under this Section 3.4.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 'dedicated telecommunications connection to TAG.' The changed text modifies this to allow LMG to choose between a dedicated or shared connection, contradicting the original requirement of a dedicated connection. This contradicts the expectation set in the initial agreement for a dedicated connection, as stated in the perturbation explanation.
